name: Leticia "Letty" Garcia
bio: Leticia 'Letty' Garcia, 42, is a nurse by profession and the current Berwyn City Clerk. She ran against Frank Aguilar in the 2022 Democratic primary, losing by about 8 points (45.7% to 54.3%). In 2025, she ran as an independent and was elected City Clerk of Berwyn. She is presenting herself as the grassroots progressive in the three-way 2026 primary, contrasting with both the incumbent Aguilar and well-funded challenger Miranda Hernandez. According to media reports, she has raised less than $10,000 for her campaign. She is also simultaneously on the ballot running for Democratic State Central Committeewoman from the 4th Congressional District against incumbent Elizabeth 'Lisa' Hernandez — the mother of her rival Miranda Hernandez.
prior_office: Berwyn City Clerk (elected 2025, serving as of 2026)

jacket.note: Raised less than $10,000 per media reports (Riverside-Brookfield Landmark). Illinois Sunshine committee URL not confirmed. Total District 16 race funds = $214,402.83 (all three candidates).

policy_platform.position: Responsible budgeting and transparency in county spending
Increased access to health and mental health services for seniors, veterans, children, and families
Property tax relief and exemptions for homeowners and small businesses
